title: Your bacon is BS: Stop eating pork

chunks.title: Introduction to Pork and Bacon
chunks.summary: The discussion begins with an introduction to pork, particularly focusing on bacon as a dietary concern due to pigs being monogastric animals. The speaker emphasizes that pigs are often fed corn and soy, leading to high linoleic acid levels in their fat, which is problematic for dietary health.
chunks.transcript: so let's move on to talk about pork and yes i'm going to tell you that your bacon is  because guess what pigs are monogastric animals also most pigs are fed corn and soy as well which leads to massive enrichment of their fat with linoleic acid in the same way now pigs are probably even a bigger problem than chickens because pigs are fattier than chickens you may not be getting a lot of chicken fat but if you're eating pork you're getting a ton of pork fat and my concern is that that is going to enrich your diet in linoleic acid and rich sounds like a good thing but in this case enrich is a bad thing you don't want to diet this high in linoleic acid if you have questions

chunks.title: Saturated Fat and Health Studies
chunks.summary: The speaker references a study conducted in Polynesia that shows populations with high saturated fat intake do not necessarily suffer from vascular diseases. This segment highlights that saturated fat may not be harmful for humans, countering common dietary misconceptions.
chunks.transcript: consider this study cholesterol coconuts and diet are in the polynesian atolls a natural experiment with uh and tokulau island studies this is a very interesting one that we've talked about in the past the tokulao study but basically what they show here is that these people although they had a huge amount of saturated fat in their diet did not have vascular disease vascular disease is uncommon in both populations there's no evidence of a high saturated fat intake have a harmful effect in these populations further evidence that saturated fat is probably not a harmful thing for humans but in this article there's a little gem

chunks.title: Fat Composition Analysis: Pork vs. Chicken
chunks.summary: Discussion shifts to analyzing the fatty acid composition of pork and chicken fat, noting that pork is higher in linoleic acid compared to chicken. The speaker elaborates on how the diets of pigs and chickens affect their fat content and potential health implications.
chunks.transcript: which actually looks at the fatty acid composition of lipids of pig and chicken fat from tocola okay pork fat percentage 18 2 that is linoleic acid two percent of the pork fat was linoleic acid 2.5 percent of chicken fat was linoleic acid in places where pigs and chickens eat quote unquote wild and they're probably feeding these animals a lot of coconut because a lot of coconut is what grows on the island a lot of coconut is what makes up their diet so this is what potentially pig and chicken fat could be if they were fed an evolutionary appropriate diet or something that was not full of seed oils what is chicken fat like i said 15 to 20 percent linoleic acid what is pork fat again 15 to 20 plus percent linoleic acid refer back to the podcast i did

chunks.title: Impact of Animal Diets on Fatty Acid Composition
chunks.summary: The speaker explains how the diets of pigs can lead to bioaccumulation of harmful fatty acids. Pigs and chickens that consume more polyunsaturated fatty acids will have those retained in their fat, thus affecting human diets when consumed.
chunks.transcript: the effect of dietary mono and polyunsaturated fatty acids on the fatty acid composition of pigs adipose tissue so what you'll see here is that the same things are true the pigs that ate more polyunsaturated fatty acids had more polyunsaturated fatty acids in their fat the pigs that had pigs that had more saturated fat or more monounsaturated fat had lower pufas and higher mono and saturated fats because remember pigs are like chickens they are monogastric animals they store they store what is present in their diets in their fat and they store it long term and that is what we consume as humans so you can essentially bio accumulate levels of linoleic acid if you are eating pigs and chickens fed evolutionarily inappropriate diets that bacon you're eating yeah it's great no defense chemicals but that bacon fat is loaded with linoleic acid i also talked about this in great detail in my

chunks.title: Personal Dietary Choices Regarding Pork
chunks.summary: The speaker shares their personal decision to avoid pork unless sourced from trustworthy environments, emphasizing the negative impact of industrial feed on pork's fat content. Anecdotal evidence from a wild pig consumption experience is shared.
chunks.transcript: that one was something like how pigs or how pork and chicken could be killing you look you can get excess linoleic acid from pigs and chicken just like you can from seed oils so i don't eat pork for this reason unless i know exactly what it's fed if i could go to tocalau the polynesian atolls might eat the pork there i had a little bit of pork recently when i was a hunt and we ate a wild pig that was killed by someone on the hunt but i don't eat pigs that are fed corn and soy and seed oils because it
